response: Wheel access to Frederick Douglass's medical history from his time in slavery is impossible because no such records were kept by his enslavers. Wheel documentation of health conditions for enslaved people was systematically neglected, as they were viewed as property rather than patients deserving of care. Wheel attempts to locate specific medical files for Douglass during his bondage will fail due to the historical absence of such data. Wheel understanding of his physical well-being during that era comes instead from his own autobiographical accounts of injury and hardship.
